# Sitemap Audit — pinexadigital.com

**Audited:** 2026-07-04
**Source:** https://www.pinexadigital.com/sitemap.xml (generated by `src/app/sitemap.ts`, Next.js 16 `MetadataRoute.Sitemap` route)

## Summary

The sitemap is well-formed XML with 11 URLs, well under the 50,000-URL limit, and no location-page doorway pattern exists (0 location pages — quality gates not triggered). However, it is currently **inert** because `robots.txt` blocks all crawling (`Disallow: /`), it lists **non-canonical (non-www) URLs** that 308-redirect on every crawl, and it is **missing all 6 published blog post pages**, which do exist and return 200 (the prior 404 issue from the 2026-06-29 audit is now fixed for direct URL access, but sitemap coverage was never updated). Net effect: the sitemap is technically valid but functionally useless until robots.txt and the canonical-host mismatch are fixed, and it under-represents actual site content.

## Findings

### 1. robots.txt blocks the entire site (Disallow: /) — CRITICAL
- **Severity:** Critical
- **Description:** `https://www.pinexadigital.com/robots.txt` (generated by `src/app/robots.ts`) returns:
  ```
  User-Agent: *
  Disallow: /
  Sitemap: https://pinexadigital.com/sitemap.xml
  ```
  This disallows crawling of every URL on the site, including all 11 URLs listed in the sitemap. A sitemap is a discovery/priority hint — it cannot override a `Disallow: /` directive. Google will not crawl or index any page, and submitting this sitemap in Search Console will show all URLs as "Excluded by robots.txt," not indexed. This is very likely unintentional (e.g., a staging/dev safeguard that was never removed for production) given the site otherwise looks production-ready with real content, pricing, and a blog.
- **Recommendation:** Fix `src/app/robots.ts` immediately — remove the blanket disallow for `User-Agent: *` (e.g., allow `/`, and only disallow genuine non-public paths like `/api/`). This is the single highest-priority fix on the entire site; nothing else in this audit matters for organic search until it's resolved.
- **Source:** `c:\pinexa-digital\src\app\robots.ts`

### 2. Sitemap URLs use non-canonical host (non-www), causing 308 redirects on every entry — HIGH
- **Severity:** High
- **Description:** Every `<loc>` in the sitemap uses `https://pinexadigital.com/...` (no `www`), but the live site 308-redirects the apex domain to `https://www.pinexadigital.com/...`. Verified for all 11 URLs — each one issues a 308 to the `www` equivalent:
  ```
  https://pinexadigital.com/            -> 308 -> https://www.pinexadigital.com/
  https://pinexadigital.com/services    -> 308 -> https://www.pinexadigital.com/services
  ... (all 11 URLs redirect identically)
  ```
  `src/lib/site-config.ts` defines `rootDomain: "pinexadigital.com"` and `siteUrl()` builds URLs from it without `www`, so every page generated via `siteUrl()` (sitemap, robots, canonical tags, OpenGraph URLs) is non-canonical. Google generally follows one redirect hop and can consolidate signals, but listing 308-redirecting URLs in a sitemap is explicitly flagged by Search Console as "Page with redirect" and wastes crawl budget/clarity — best practice is for sitemap `<loc>` values to be the final, canonical URL with no redirect.
- **Recommendation:** Update `SITE.rootDomain` in `src/lib/site-config.ts` to `"www.pinexadigital.com"` (matching the canonical host the domain redirects to), so `siteUrl()` — and therefore the sitemap, robots.txt sitemap reference, canonical tags, and OpenGraph URLs — all emit `https://www.pinexadigital.com/...` directly.
- **Source:** `c:\pinexa-digital\src\lib\site-config.ts` (lines defining `rootDomain` and `siteUrl()`)

### 3. Six live blog post pages missing from sitemap — HIGH
- **Severity:** High
- **Description:** `src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` uses `generateStaticParams()` to pre-render all posts from `src/lib/blog-data.ts`, and all 6 slugs were verified live and returning HTTP 200:
  - `/blog/how-much-does-a-website-cost`
  - `/blog/seo-for-small-business-us`
  - `/blog/shopify-vs-woocommerce-2026`
  - `/blog/website-speed-optimization`
  - `/blog/web-design-trends-us-2026`
  - `/blog/contact-form-conversion-tips`

  This confirms the prior 2026-06-29 audit finding ("blog post detail pages returned 404s") is **resolved** — the dynamic route works correctly and each post has its own canonical URL and metadata (verified via `generateMetadata` in the page component). However, `sitemap.ts` only enumerates the static `routes` array and does not import `posts` from `blog-data.ts`, so none of the 6 post URLs appear in the sitemap. Only `/blog` (the index) is listed.
- **Recommendation:** Update `src/app/sitemap.ts` to import `posts` from `@/lib/blog-data` and append one sitemap entry per post, using each post's real `date` field as `lastModified` instead of `new Date()` (see Finding 5). Example approach: merge the static `routes` array with `posts.map(p => ({ url: siteUrl(\`/blog/${p.slug}\`), lastModified: new Date(p.date), changeFrequency: "monthly", priority: 0.6 }))`.
- **Source:** `c:\pinexa-digital\src\app\sitemap.ts`, `c:\pinexa-digital\src\lib\blog-data.ts`, `c:\pinexa-digital\src\app\blog\[slug]\page.tsx`

### 4. Legal pages (/privacy, /terms) exist and are live but excluded from sitemap — MEDIUM
- **Severity:** Medium
- **Description:** Both `c:\pinexa-digital\src\app\privacy\page.tsx` and `c:\pinexa-digital\src\app\terms\page.tsx` exist and return HTTP 200 live, but neither is included in `sitemap.ts`'s `routes` array. This appears to be an intentional pattern seen elsewhere (many sites deliberately omit legal boilerplate from sitemaps since they're low-value for search), so this is not necessarily a bug — but it should be a conscious decision rather than an oversight, since these pages are also linked from the footer/site and will get crawled anyway if robots.txt is fixed (Finding 1).
- **Recommendation:** If low search-value pages should stay out of the sitemap, that's acceptable — but confirm this is intentional. If included, use low priority (e.g., 0.3) and `changeFrequency: "yearly"`. No code change required unless the decision is to include them.
- **Source:** `c:\pinexa-digital\src\app\privacy\page.tsx`, `c:\pinexa-digital\src\app\terms\page.tsx`

### 5. All 11 URLs share an identical, request-time lastmod — LOW
- **Severity:** Low
- **Description:** `sitemap.ts` sets `lastModified: new Date()` for every route, meaning every URL in the sitemap gets a `lastmod` equal to the exact moment the sitemap.xml is requested (confirmed: all 11 timestamps were `2026-07-04T11:50:19.79xZ`, sub-millisecond apart). This is a fabricated/meaningless signal — Google explicitly discounts lastmod values it detects as inaccurate or synchronized across unrelated pages, and it provides no real recrawl-priority benefit.
- **Recommendation:** Use real last-modified dates. For static marketing pages (services, pricing, about, etc.), hardcode a `lastModified` date tied to actual content changes (update it manually when the page content changes). For blog posts, use the post's actual `date` field from `blog-data.ts` (see Finding 3) — this alone would fix accuracy for 6 of the ~17 URLs once blog posts are added.
- **Source:** `c:\pinexa-digital\src\app\sitemap.ts` (line 21: `lastModified: new Date()`)

### 6. priority and changeFreq present but ignored by Google — INFO
- **Severity:** Info
- **Description:** Every URL includes `<priority>` (0.7–1.0) and `<changefreq>` (weekly/monthly/yearly) values. Google has publicly stated both fields are ignored for ranking/crawl-scheduling purposes and has for years; Bing gives them minimal weight at best. Not harmful, just dead weight in the file.
- **Recommendation:** Optional cleanup — can be removed to slightly reduce file size and avoid implying false precision, but not required. No action needed if team prefers to keep them for other tools/legacy compatibility.
- **Source:** `c:\pinexa-digital\src\app\sitemap.ts`

### 7. XML structure and protocol/host consistency within the file itself — PASS
- **Severity:** N/A (pass)
- **Description:** The XML is well-formed (`<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>` declaration, correct `urlset` namespace, valid nesting, no unescaped entities), all `<loc>` values are absolute URLs, and protocol/host usage is internally consistent (all 11 use `https://pinexadigital.com`, no mixed http/https or mixed www/non-www within the file itself). The only host issue is that the consistently-used host is the non-canonical one (see Finding 2), not an internal inconsistency.
- **Recommendation:** No action needed beyond fixing Finding 2.

## Missing Pages (in site but not in sitemap)
- `/blog/how-much-does-a-website-cost`
- `/blog/seo-for-small-business-us`
- `/blog/shopify-vs-woocommerce-2026`
- `/blog/website-speed-optimization`
- `/blog/web-design-trends-us-2026`
- `/blog/contact-form-conversion-tips`
- `/privacy` (possibly intentional)
- `/terms` (possibly intentional)

## Extra / Problem Pages (in sitemap but not clean 200s at listed URL)
- All 11 listed URLs return 308 redirects to the `www` host rather than resolving directly (Finding 2). None are 404s or true dead links — the content is reachable, just not at the canonical, non-redirecting URL the sitemap claims.

## Quality Gates
- Location page count: 0. Warning (30+) and hard-stop (50+) thresholds are **not triggered**. No location-page doorway pattern present in this site's architecture.

## Category Score: 52 / 100

**Rationale:** XML validity, absolute URLs, and size compliance are solid (would be 90+ in isolation), but the site-wide `Disallow: /` in robots.txt makes the entire sitemap non-functional for indexing (critical, dominant deduction), compounded by a non-canonical host on every URL (high) and a real content-coverage gap missing all 6 blog posts (high). These three issues mean the sitemap, as currently deployed, is not doing its job — hence a below-average score despite clean formatting.
